page: Amazon Web Services d2.4xlarge is a dense storage instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor. It features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 122.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB per core. A key hardware feature is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 24,576 GB of local HDD storage, coupled with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. The instance runs on the Xen hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as shown by stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. Memory read bandwidth is average at 625.97 GB/sec for cached blocks and 44.35 GB/sec for uncached blocks, though large block write bandwidth is weaker, placing in the bottom 25%. Cryptographic and integer operations benefit from AVX, AES, and FMA instruction sets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d2.4xlarge offers a high density of local HDD storage relative to its compute and memory resources, making it a viable option for sequential read/write storage workloads where high-capacity local storage is prioritized over high-speed NVMe or SSD performance. Given the weak single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities, this instance is best suited for storage-intensive workloads such as MapReduce jobs, distributed file systems, log processing, and large-scale data warehousing.
description: Amazon Web Services d2.4xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for high-capacity local storage workloads. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 122.0 GB of memory. The instance is equipped with 24,576 GB of local HDD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data shows poor single-core performance, but average multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth capabilities. While write bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks are relatively weak, the instance provides a high volume of local storage per resource unit. It is best suited for storage-intensive applications, distributed file systems, log processing, and data warehousing workloads that require massive sequential disk throughput without the need for high-speed SSDs or intensive single-core compute performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d2.4xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 122.0 GB of memory, and 24,576 GB of local HDD storage, designed for high-capacity sequential data workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d2.4xlarge is a dense storage instance with 16 vCPUs, 122.0 GB RAM, and 24,576 GB of local HDD storage for data-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity dense storage instance featuring massive local HDD capacity and balanced multi-core performance for sequential data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 122.0 GB of system memory
24,576 GB of local HDD storage capacity
Intel Xeon E5-2676 v3 processor running at 2.4 GHz
Well-suited for MapReduce, HDFS, and large-scale log processing
